commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an OpenCode Zen provider catalog module, manifest model rows, runtime discovery/filtering, base-URL normalization, shared catalog credential lookup, and provider tests.

PR surface: Source +1900, Tests +403. Total +2303 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main lacks Zen modelCatalog data plus catalog/augment/resolve hooks, so selecting a non-default opencode/... model without manual provider rows reproduces the reported model_not_found path by source inspection.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can resolve the docs/catalog mismatch; seed scope, shared credentials, and CI rerun still need maintainer or normal merge handling afterward.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the bundled `opencode` Zen provider lacked the provider-owned catalog/resolver that its sibling `opencode-go` already had, so users had to hand-register Zen models and routes; review also showed `minimax-m2.7` was being sent to the wrong Zen transport.
- Solution: restore the Zen provider catalog, dynamic resolver, catalog augmentation, route normalization, manifest metadata, and required model cost metadata at the `opencode` plugin boundary.
- What changed: the PR adds the Zen static seed, runtime discovery, family-specific routing, and tests; this update filters live discovery to provider-owned metadata rows only, routes MiniMax through the documented Zen OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint, and reconciles all 48 static seed rows with the existing required `ModelDefinitionConfig.cost` contract. Tiered public-doc rows now use `tieredPricing` instead of being flattened or omitted.
- What did NOT change: this follow-up does not change non-OpenCode providers, `opencode-go` runtime behavior, generic LLM adapter APIs, the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy, or the public model/config cost contract.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can select advertised `opencode/<model>` Zen models without hand-writing every model row, while ID-only live discovery can no longer make unknown rows selectable with guessed metadata and MiniMax no longer hits the failing Anthropic Messages path.
- Fixes #92479

## Origin / follow-up

- **Follows / completes:** This updates the existing #92495 repair branch after ClawSweeper flagged live-only Zen row synthesis, missing successful MiniMax live proof, and the final cost-contract type failure in the same PR.
- **Gap this fills:** The previous PR head restored the catalog but still synthesized full metadata for live IDs missing from the static seed, grouped MiniMax with the Zen Anthropic Messages route, and then tried to omit unverified cost fields even though the current model/config contract requires `cost` on every model row.
- **Consistency:** The live-discovery behavior matches the shared `buildLiveModelProviderConfig` pattern used by `opencode-go`: filter to provider-owned metadata rows and fall back to the static seed when discovery is unavailable or yields no owned rows. The MiniMax route now matches the documented Zen `/v1/chat/completions` behavior proven with a redacted Zen key. Cost metadata now preserves the existing required-cost contract instead of widening the model/config schema.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #92495 is the existing linked PR for #92479; `check-upstream-fixed` found `origin/main` does not already cover this repair.
- **Related open PR / same-contract scan:** The linked issue and ClawSweeper review identify this PR as the active candidate fix. I did not find a same-contract canonical PR on `origin/main` superseding the Zen catalog owner-boundary repair.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** The current linked PR itself had author-actionable code gaps: live-only discovery rows were exposed with inferred route/input/pricing/context/max-token metadata, MiniMax was routed through the Zen Anthropic Messages endpoint that returned 401 for the provided valid Zen key, and unverified static seed rows violated the current required model cost contract.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** It fixes those gaps at the provider-owned catalog/routing boundary instead of adding downstream special cases, publishing guessed live-only rows, or widening the shared model schema from one provider PR.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The focused provider test exercises live discovery with a known row plus unknown row, an unknown-only discovery response, the static cost contract, and the route normalization path. A redacted live request also proves the current `minimax-m2.7` route returns successful model output.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a static seed visible when live discovery is unavailable or has no owned rows, filters live-only IDs until metadata is provider-owned, resolves shared OpenCode credentials for the Zen catalog, routes Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters, and keeps every static Zen row within the required model cost contract. The MiniMax route is `openai-completions` at `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`, which maps to the documented `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions` endpoint.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw PR worktree. The earlier MiniMax live proof used a redacted OpenCode Zen API key only as a transient environment variable; the raw key is not printed, stored, or included here. The latest cost-contract update used public Zen docs plus local source/type/package-boundary verification; no Zen key was available in the current shell for a fresh authenticated `/models` rerun.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
export EVIDENCE_DIR TASK_WORKTREE
cd "$TASK_WORKTREE"

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-cost-contract-green-20260621.log" 2>&1

pnpm run tsgo:prod \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-tsgo-prod-cost-contract-20260621.log" 2>&1

pnpm run check:test-types \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-check-test-types-cost-contract-20260621.log" 2>&1

pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-package-boundary-compile-cost-contract-20260621.log" 2>&1

pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-package-boundary-canary-cost-contract-20260621.log" 2>&1

node --input-type=module -e 'import fs from "node:fs"; import manifest from "./extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json" with { type: "json" }; const models = manifest.modelCatalog.providers.opencode.models; const missingCost = models.filter((model) => !model.cost).map((model) => model.id); const tiered = models.filter((model) => Array.isArray(model.cost?.tieredPricing)).map((model) => model.id); const freeRows = models.filter((model) => model.cost && Object.values({ input: model.cost.input, output: model.cost.output, cacheRead: model.cost.cacheRead, cacheWrite: model.cost.cacheWrite }).every((value) => value === 0)).map((model) => model.id); fs.writeFileSync(process.env.EVIDENCE_DIR + "/opencode-cost-contract-summary-20260621.json", `${JSON.stringify({ total: models.length, missingCost, tiered, freeRows }, null, 2)}\n`);'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** The focused provider regression test passes with the new cost-contract assertion over every static manifest/runtime row:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 20.27s
```

The two type gates that failed on the previous head now pass:

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:extensions
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

The extension package-boundary check that failed on the previous head now passes in both compile and canary modes:

```text
extension package boundary check passed
mode: compile
compiled plugins: 115
canary plugins: 0
```

```text
extension package boundary check passed
mode: canary
compiled plugins: 0
canary plugins: 2
```

The manifest summary shows the 48-row seed now has no missing `cost` fields, with tiered pricing preserved for public-doc tiered rows and the free rows explicit:

```json
{
  "total": 48,
  "missingCost": [],
  "tiered": [
    "claude-sonnet-4-5",
    "claude-sonnet-4",
    "gemini-3.1-pro",
    "gpt-5.5",
    "gpt-5.4"
  ],
  "freeRows": [
    "big-pickle",
    "deepseek-v4-flash-free",
    "mimo-v2.5-free",
    "qwen3.6-plus-free",
    "minimax-m3-free",
    "nemotron-3-ultra-free",
    "north-mini-code-free"
  ]
}
```

The public Zen docs pricing sample used for the cost refresh includes the representative flat, tiered, and free rows:

```text
MiniMax M2.7 $0.30 $1.20 $0.06 $0.375
Qwen3.6 Plus $0.50 $3.00 $0.05 $0.625
Qwen3.5 Plus $0.20 $1.20 $0.02 $0.25
Claude Opus 4.7 $5.00 $25.00 $0.50 $6.25
Claude Sonnet 4.5 (≤ 200K tokens) $3.00 $15.00 $0.30 $3.75
Claude Sonnet 4.5 (> 200K tokens) $6.00 $22.50 $0.60 $7.50
Gemini 3.1 Pro (≤ 200K tokens) $2.00 $12.00 $0.20 -
Gemini 3.1 Pro (> 200K tokens) $4.00 $18.00 $0.40 -
GPT 5.5 (≤ 272K tokens) $5.00 $30.00 $0.50 -
GPT 5.5 (> 272K tokens) $10.00 $45.00 $1.00 -
GPT 5 Nano $0.05 $0.40 $0.005 -
DeepSeek V4 Flash Free Free Free Free -
MiMo-V2.5 Free Free Free Free -
North Mini Code Free Free Free Free -
Nemotron 3 Ultra Free Free Free Free -
```

The previous authenticated MiniMax route proof remains valid for the current route shape:

```text
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=openai-completions
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1
current_route_endpoint=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
documented_endpoint=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
current_route_matches_documented=true
request_auth=Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]
curl_exit_status=0
response_status=200
response_ok=true
response_type=chat.completion
response_model=accounts/fireworks/models/minimax-m2p7
response_finish_reason=stop
response_content_preview=OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK
response_error=null
secret-scan=pass
current-route-live-proof-exit-status=0
```

Earlier authenticated proof also covered discovery and representative live completions through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax/OpenAI-compatible paths:

```text
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed Zen Anthropic Messages path, the current MiniMax route matches the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint, live discovery no longer makes ID-only unknown Zen rows selectable with guessed metadata, and every static seed row now carries a `cost` object compatible with the current model/config contract. Tiered public-doc rows use `tieredPricing`; free rows are explicit zero-cost rows rather than accidental default fallbacks.
- **What was not tested:** I did not re-run authenticated Zen `/models` discovery for this latest cost-only follow-up because no Zen key is available in the current shell. The previous redacted proof still shows `authenticated_discovery_models=48` for this seed. I did not expand the seed to newly visible public-doc rows or remove older authenticated seed rows such as `qwen3.6-plus-free` / `minimax-m3-free`; changing that seed membership remains a provider-owner catalog decision. I also did not change or prove the provider-owner policy decisions for the full static seed or the shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — `status: ⏳ waiting on author`:** Addressed for author-actionable code by fixing the blocking cost-contract/type failure and refreshing local evidence. This should be cleared by the next ClawSweeper review if no new author-actionable findings remain.
- **RF-002 — provider-owner acceptance of 48-row seed and shared credential fallback:** Disclosed as a maintainer/provider-owner decision. This patch keeps the current 48-row candidate seed and does not silently change the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- **RF-003 — 48-row static seed drift / user-visible metadata risk:** Improved by reconciling every current seed row with the required cost contract and public Zen docs pricing where available, including tiered rows. Remaining seed-membership drift is disclosed rather than hidden: the current shell cannot refresh authenticated live IDs, and seed acceptance remains provider-owner scope.
- **RF-004 — split-key policy overlap:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The fallback mirrors the sibling OpenCode Go catalog path and stays bounded to OpenCode catalog loading, but overlaps the open split-key policy discussion.
- **RF-005 — cost fix contract decision:** Fixed by choosing the conservative option: preserve the current required `ModelDefinitionConfig.cost` contract and fill all Zen rows, instead of widening the model/config contract from this provider repair PR.
- **RF-006 — keep Zen rows within the model cost contract:** Fixed. `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts` now resolves a required cost for every static Zen row, `extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json` is synchronized from that provider-owned config, and `opencode-index-cost-contract-green-20260621.log`, `opencode-tsgo-prod-cost-contract-20260621.log`, `opencode-check-test-types-cost-contract-20260621.log`, plus package-boundary compile/canary logs all pass.
- **Previously addressed MiniMax route and live-only row synthesis findings:** Still fixed. MiniMax M2.7 remains on the OpenAI-compatible chat completions route, and `buildDiscoveredOpencodeZenModels` still filters live-only rows through the provider-owned static seed.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus type/package-boundary gates and redacted live evidence stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 static model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs, live-only discovery rows are filtered until metadata is owned, every static manifest/runtime row has required cost metadata, and static fallback remains visible when live discovery is unavailable or returns no owned IDs.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing/cost contract without broad adapter mocks, while the live proof covers the external provider boundary for the exact MiniMax route ClawSweeper requested.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. This update reduces the pricing-specific risk by preserving the required cost contract and encoding public-doc tiered pricing instead of omitting costs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly static/generated catalog metadata rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The MiniMax proof risk is reduced by the redacted successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion on the current route; provider-owner review is still needed for the seed/auth policy boundaries.
- **Why acceptable:** The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, prevents live-only ID metadata synthesis, preserves a no-discovery local seed, fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review, and keeps that seed compatible with the existing cost schema.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. Zen live discovery also returns ID-only rows, so unowned IDs cannot safely become selectable rich model rows without provider-owned metadata. Separately, MiniMax was grouped with Claude/Qwen on the Zen Anthropic Messages route even though live Zen behavior proves MiniMax runs through the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions route. The latest broken head then tried to remove unverified cost metadata by omitting `cost`, but the current `ModelDefinitionConfig` contract requires `cost` on every model row.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary, keeps rich metadata owned by the provider seed, fixes MiniMax route selection before downstream adapter execution, and preserves the existing required cost contract with public-doc pricing/tiered metadata instead of adding downstream string special cases, guessed live-only metadata, or a schema-wide optional-cost change.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause provider catalog, routing, and model-cost contract repair for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the author-actionable source repair: the catalog regression, live-only synthesis finding, MiniMax route proof request, and final cost-contract type failure are now covered by focused provider tests, type gates, package-boundary checks, public docs cost evidence, and redacted live route evidence.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback/default warnings are expected catalog-boundary behavior, not symptom masking: live model catalogs are advisory, and provider-owned static rows remain visible when discovery is unavailable or returns no owned metadata rows. The large diff is mostly static/generated catalog metadata needed to restore the regressed Zen catalog. Out of scope: changing non-OpenCode providers, generic adapter APIs, the sibling `opencode-go` runtime, the full seed-membership policy, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. This changes catalog/routing/cost metadata before downstream adapter selection rather than adding mirrored/persisted records or generic runtime fallbacks.
target.author: mushuiyu886
